message:
feat(update): add Sigstore signature verification for release artifacts

Extends the update system to verify cryptographic signatures on release
binaries using Sigstore/cosign bundles, providing supply chain security
beyond simple checksum verification.

New functionality:
- DownloadedRelease now tracks signature_verified: Option<bool>
- SignatureVerificationFailed error variant for signature failures
- verify_checksum_and_signature() combines checksum + optional signature
- verify_signature() invokes cosign CLI to verify .sigstore.json bundles

Download flow changes:
- Look for {archive}.sigstore.json alongside checksums
- Download signature bundle if available
- Pass bundle path to combined verification function
- Report signature status in DownloadedRelease result

Verification implementation:
- Uses which::which to locate cosign in PATH
- Gracefully degrades if cosign not installed (returns error)
- Processes Sigstore bundle format (not detached .sig files)
- Logs warning when no bundle available (checksum-only mode)

This enables end-to-end verification when releases are signed with
GitHub Actions' keyless signing via Sigstore's Fulcio CA, ensuring
binaries weren't tampered with between build and download.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
